title: Validate Binary Search Tree
problem_statement: Determine whether a binary tree is a valid binary search tree.

examples.explanation: Every node satisfies BST ordering relative to its ancestors.

constraints: The number of nodes is in the range [1, 10^4]
intuition: Use recursion with value bounds to ensure every node is within the allowed range.

approaches.name: Bounded recursion
approaches.description: Pass minimum and maximum allowed values during recursion and validate each node against them.
approaches.why_not: Inorder traversal comparison also works but needs extra tracking of previous value.

interview_qa.q: What bounds are passed to children?
interview_qa.a: The left child gets the current node’s value as an upper bound, the right child gets it as a lower bound.

interview_qa.q: Why is this better than simple subtree checks?
interview_qa.a: It ensures all descendants respect all ancestor constraints.
